articles.title: This Is a Phase to Accumulate Aggressively: Sailesh Raj Bhan on Where Smart Money Is Looking
articles.description:
Will markets revisit the March lows? Where are the best opportunities for long-term investors today?
In an exclusive conversation on The Wealth Formula with N Mahalakshmi, Sailesh Raj Bhan, President & CIO – Equity Investments, Nippon India Mutual Fund, shares his market outlook, investment philosophy and framework for identifying businesses that can compound wealth over long periods.
He discusses the impact of oil prices on markets, lessons from a 30-year investing journey, the importance of not overpaying for growth, why sustainability of earnings matters more than narratives, and sectors where expectations remain low despite attractive fundamentals.
Key topics include:
Chances of markets retesting the March lows
Growth versus valuation
The role of PEG ratios in stock selection
Private sector banks and consumer stocks
Pharma and generic opportunities
Evaluating business quality and free cash flow
Navigating volatility and market noise
Building a high-quality portfolio for the next 3–5 years
Watch the full interview for insights on how one of India's most experienced fund managers is navigating today's market environment.

articles.title: Stock market today: US futures rise as Iran peace talks progress ahead of inflation report
articles.description: US stock futures rose as Iran peace talks eased tensions, oil prices fell, and investors awaited key PCE inflation data and Micron earnings this week.

articles.title: Dow, S&P 500 rise at open; Nasdaq lags as traders weigh Iran talks
articles.description: US stocks opened mixed, with the Dow and S&P 500 higher while the Nasdaq lagged, as investors weighed progress in US-Iran peace talks, easing oil prices, and upcoming US inflation data. Sentiment was also shaped by AI demand optimism and reactions to the death of former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan.

articles.title: When Will Gasoline Prices Return to Pre-War Levels?
articles.description: Falling crude and gasoline prices reflect optimism about a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but depleted inventories, restocking demand, and lingering logistical constraints could slow the return to pre-war fuel prices.

articles.title: Oil Continues To Drop As U.S. Treasury Authorizes Iranian Oil Sales
articles.description: Oil prices continued to drop on Monday following progress in talks between the U.S. and Iran and the Treasury Department's authorization of Iranian oil sales under a 60-day license.

articles.title: US stocks drift near their records after oil prices fall
articles.description: U.S. stocks are drifting near their records after trading resumed following a three-day weekend

articles.title: India’s ethanol dream hinges on affordability, not policy push
articles.description: Ethanol may need to fall to around ₹60-65 a litre before India’s E100 push wins mass-market adoption, according to industry executives, analysts and automakers who say consumer economics, not government policy, will determine whether motorists switch from petrol. As policymakers look beyond blending targets towards direct ethanol use in vehicles, a new challenge is emerging — creating consumer demand

articles.title: US stocks hold near their records as trading resumes on Wall Street after a holiday weekend
articles.description: U.S. stocks are drifting near their records after trading resumed following a three-day weekend for Wall Street. The S&P 500 edged up 0.2% early Monday. The index is coming off its 11th winning week in the last 12. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 204 points, and the Nasdaq composite was down 0.3%. Crude oil prices eased following talks over the weekend between the United States and Iran on their war. The price of Brent crude fell 2.6%. Treasury yields rose in the bond market. European markets were mostly higher and Asian markets were mixed.

articles.title: Baltimore drivers see gas prices rise 4.6 cents, GasBuddy reports
